description: Career archetype definitions with detection criteria. Used by the strategy-advisor agent to classify candidates and guide strategic recommendations.

linear_progression.name: Linear Progression
linear_progression.description: Candidate with steady upward career growth within one industry or domain. Shows clear advancement through progressively senior roles.

patterns: Multiple roles at the same company showing promotion
Job titles that show increasing seniority over time
Consistent industry or domain across all roles
Steady increase in scope (team size, budget, responsibility)
structural_indicators: 5+ years of experience in a single domain
2+ roles with the same employer
No gaps longer than 3 months between roles
Education aligns with career field
Job titles follow a clear progression ladder

weaknesses_to_mitigate: May appear too narrow or specialized if targeting a broader role
Could seem risk-averse or lacking breadth
Multiple roles at one company may raise questions about adaptability

career_changer.name: Career Changer
career_changer.description: Candidate transitioning from one field or industry to a different one. Prior experience may not directly match the target role.

patterns: Recent education or certifications in a different field than prior work
Summary mentions transition or career change
Most recent role is in a different industry than earlier roles
Projects section showcases skills from the new field
Skills section emphasizes new-field competencies
structural_indicators: Industry mismatch between recent and older roles
Recent bootcamp, degree, or certification in a new field
Projects section features work in the target field
Experience in the target field is less than 2 years
Skills listed do not match the majority of prior job titles

key_strengths_to_emphasize: Transferable skills that bridge old and new fields
Demonstrated ability to learn and adapt quickly
Relevant projects, coursework, or certifications in target field
Domain knowledge that enriches the new role
weaknesses_to_mitigate: Lack of direct experience in the target field
Job titles that do not match the target role
Potential perception of being a flight risk or uncommitted
Gaps in domain-specific technical depth

return_to_work.name: Return to Work
return_to_work.description: Candidate re-entering the workforce after a career gap of 1 or more years due to caregiving, health, personal reasons, or other circumstances.

patterns: Unexplained gap of 1+ years between the most recent role and the present
Freelance or consulting work listed to cover a gap period
Volunteer work or community involvement during the gap
Recent upskilling activities (courses, certifications) before re-entry
Summary explicitly addresses the career break
structural_indicators: Most recent role ended 1+ years ago with no subsequent employment
Gap of 1+ years between two consecutive roles
Presence of volunteer, freelance, or training entries during the gap
Recent certifications or courses suggesting skill refreshing
Resume date range shows an unexplained break in employment

key_strengths_to_emphasize: Skills maintained or refreshed during the break
Freelance, consulting, or volunteer work during the gap
Recent training or certifications showing current competence
Maturity and perspective gained during the break
weaknesses_to_mitigate: Career gap raises questions about skill currency
Technology or industry changes during the absence
Potential bias against candidates with employment gaps
Reduced professional network from time away

entry_level.name: Entry Level
entry_level.description: Candidate with under 2 years of professional experience, typically a recent graduate or early career professional.

patterns: Education section is more detailed than experience section
Projects section features academic or personal projects
Experience consists primarily of internships or part-time roles
Summary uses language like 'aspiring', 'passionate', 'eager to learn'
Graduation date is within the last 2 years
structural_indicators: Less than 2 years of total professional experience
0-2 full-time roles listed
Education section includes GPA, coursework, or honors
Multiple internship entries
Resume is 1 page or less
Presence of academic projects, hackathons, or student organizations

key_strengths_to_emphasize: Leadership in student organizations or extracurriculars
weaknesses_to_mitigate: Limited professional experience
Lack of industry-specific track record
No management or leadership experience in professional settings
Potential over-reliance on academic credentials

executive.name: Executive
executive.description: Director-level or above with 15+ years of experience. Focus is on leadership scope, strategic impact, and organizational transformation.

patterns: Job titles include Director, VP, SVP, C-level, or Head of
Achievements reference revenue, P&L, organizational scope, or headcount
Experience spans 15+ years with progressively senior roles
Board memberships, advisory roles, or speaking engagements listed
Summary positions candidate as a strategic leader
structural_indicators: 15+ years of professional experience
At least one Director-level or above title
Achievements include dollar amounts in millions or billions
Team sizes of 50+ people managed
Resume is 2+ pages
Presence of board, advisory, or industry leadership roles

weaknesses_to_mitigate: Resume may be too long with excessive detail on older roles
Could appear over-qualified for non-executive positions
Technical skills may appear outdated if not maintained
May need to demonstrate hands-on capability for smaller organizations

military_transitioner.name: Military Transitioner
military_transitioner.description: Candidate transitioning from active military service to civilian employment. Military experience requires translation to civilian-equivalent language.

patterns: Job titles include military ranks (Captain, Sergeant, Lieutenant, etc.)
Experience entries reference military bases, units, or commands
Achievements described using military terminology and acronyms
Security clearance level mentioned
Training and certifications from military institutions
Dates of service with branch identification
structural_indicators: Employer is a branch of the military
Military ranks used as job titles
Security clearance mentioned in summary or skills
Military-specific certifications or training
MOS, AFSC, or NEC codes referenced
Multiple deployments or duty station changes

key_strengths_to_emphasize: Leadership experience (often leading large teams at young ages)
Discipline, reliability, and mission-focused work ethic
Security clearances (highly valued in defense and government sectors)
Training and certifications from rigorous military programs
Experience operating in high-pressure and complex environments
Project management, logistics, and operations expertise
weaknesses_to_mitigate: Military jargon and acronyms that civilians do not understand
Job titles that have no direct civilian equivalent
Perception that military experience does not translate to corporate settings
Potential culture mismatch concerns from hiring managers
May lack civilian industry-specific experience or certifications

international_candidate.name: International Candidate
international_candidate.description: Candidate applying from or relocating to a different country. May face work authorization concerns, credential recognition, and cultural resume convention differences.

patterns: Education from institutions in foreign countries
Work experience at companies in multiple countries
Language proficiency section listing multiple languages
Address or location in a different country than the target job market
Resume includes personal information common in other countries (date of birth, nationality, photo)
Work authorization or visa status mentioned
structural_indicators: Educational institutions are international (non-domestic)
Work history spans multiple countries
Language skills section present with multiple languages
Resume includes elements uncommon in the target country (photo, DOB, marital status)
Visa or work authorization status explicitly mentioned
Resume length or format follows conventions of another country

key_strengths_to_emphasize: Experience working across different markets and regulatory environments
Adaptability demonstrated by working in multiple countries
weaknesses_to_mitigate: Work authorization uncertainty may deter employers
Foreign credentials may not be recognized or understood
Resume conventions from other countries may seem unusual
Potential language or communication concerns
Unfamiliarity with local industry norms or business culture

detection_rules.scoring_method: Each archetype is scored from 0 to 1 based on the proportion and strength of matching signals. Keyword matches carry less weight individually than pattern or structural indicator matches.
detection_rules.primary_threshold: Highest scoring archetype becomes primary if confidence >= 0.3
detection_rules.secondary_threshold: Second-highest scoring archetype becomes secondary if confidence >= 0.4
detection_rules.multi_archetype_handling: When a secondary archetype is detected, use the primary archetype's format recommendation but apply the secondary archetype's mitigation strategies as supplementary advice.
